summary: Implements the KeystoneReconciler (CC-0013) with 6 sequential sub-reconcilers (secrets, database, fernet keys, config, deployment, bootstrap), main.go wiring with external scheme registration, comprehensive unit tests (47 tests, all passing), and reference documentation. The implementation correctly follows the architecture doc, uses shared libraries from internal/common/, and produces a working reconciliation loop that drives a Keystone CR through all phases to a fully operational state.

tests_checklist.item: Error paths tested (API errors, job failures)
Edge cases covered (stale jobs, brownfield mode, min keys floor)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns in internal/common/

security_checklist.item: DB credentials read from Secrets (not hardcoded)
Admin password injected via SecretKeyRef (not plaintext)
Volume mounts are ReadOnly where appropriate

architecture_checklist.item: Sequential sub-reconciler orchestration with early return
main.go registers external schemes and instantiates reconciler (REQ-009)
RBAC markers cover all required resource groups (REQ-010)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Shared libraries used for K8s interactions

fail_fast_checklist.item: Errors returned immediately on API failures

defensive_checklist.item: DB port defaults to 3306 when not set

architecture_findings.description: Recorder field (events.EventRecorder) is declared on KeystoneReconciler struct and initialized in main.go and all tests, but is never called in any production code. This is dead code. The corresponding RBAC marker for events (groups=core,resources=events) is also missing, which would cause runtime failures if someone attempts to use the Recorder without adding the RBAC marker first.
architecture_findings.fix: Either (a) remove the Recorder field and all initializations if events are not planned for the initial implementation, or (b) add at least one event emission (e.g., on Ready=True or on error) and add the missing RBAC marker: +kubebuilder:rbac:groups=core,resources=events,verbs=create;patch

issues_found.description: reconcileSecrets does NOT set ObservedGeneration on any of its 3 condition-setting calls (lines 33, 49, 58). All other sub-reconcilers (reconcileDatabase, reconcileFernetKeys, reconcileDeployment, reconcileBootstrap) consistently set ObservedGeneration: keystone.Generation on every condition. This is a pattern inconsistency — the same concept (setting conditions) is implemented differently in one file vs all others.
issues_found.fix: Add ObservedGeneration: keystone.Generation to all 3 metav1.Condition literals in reconcileSecrets: lines 33-38 (WaitingForDBCredentials), lines 49-55 (WaitingForAdminCredentials), and lines 58-62 (SecretsAvailable).

suggested_improvements: Consider URL-encoding the database username and password when building the connection string in reconcileConfig (reconcile_config.go:81-82). Special characters in credentials (e.g., @, :, /) could break the PyMySQL connection URL. Use net/url.PathEscape or a dedicated connection string builder.
next_steps: Fix PC3-1: Add ObservedGeneration to all conditions in reconcileSecrets
Decide whether to keep or remove the unused Recorder field (Q7 finding)

detected_patterns.name: Sub-reconciler condition contract with ObservedGeneration
detected_patterns.applies_when: Setting status conditions in any sub-reconciler method of a KeystoneReconciler (or future operator reconcilers following the same pattern)
detected_patterns.description: Every sub-reconciler sets conditions via conditions.SetCondition with all 5 fields: Type (string matching the sub-reconciler's responsibility, e.g. 'DatabaseReady'), Status (metav1.ConditionTrue/False), ObservedGeneration (keystone.Generation), Reason (CamelCase action state, e.g. 'WaitingForDatabase'), and Message (human-readable description). The ObservedGeneration field MUST be set on every condition to track which CR generation the condition reflects. On requeue, the condition is set to False with an appropriate Reason before returning. On success, the condition is set to True.

examples.code:
conditions.SetCondition(&keystone.Status.Conditions, metav1.Condition{
	Type:               "DatabaseReady",
	Status:             metav1.ConditionFalse,
	ObservedGeneration: keystone.Generation,
	Reason:             "WaitingForDatabase",
	Message:            "MariaDB Database CR is not ready",
})

conditions.SetCondition(&keystone.Status.Conditions, metav1.Condition{
	Type:               "BootstrapReady",
	Status:             metav1.ConditionTrue,
	ObservedGeneration: keystone.Generation,
	Reason:             "BootstrapComplete",
	Message:            "Keystone bootstrap completed successfully",
})
